engine_source: gnu.in-os/engine/gnuin-compose-core (authoritative; port-data mirrors it — see PARITY.md)

host.motion_driver: per-surface frame-tick adoption of the engine motion: MotionDriver{ open/dismiss (retarget via interrupt_motion), tick(dt), sample(), is_animating() } — exactly Central's menu-lifecycle wiring; 5 tests
host.menu_state: pure overlay lifecycle SM enforcing AT-MOST-ONE surface: open(replace)/configure(promote;no-op-if-dismissed)/dismiss(zero-all)/on_hit(MenuRow->Action,Panel/Blob->KeepOpen,None->Dismiss)/surface_action(Reuse same-output|Recreate) — 8 tests
host.protocol: newline-delimited JSON over a Unix socket (legacy UI ContextMenuService ↔ Rust host): HostMessage{Open{request,screen}|Close} · Envelope{version,#[flatten] message} (version-negotiated, is_supported) · HostEvent{Action{id}}; canonical wire frames in scenes/wire-protocol.json (TRANSITIONAL — bridge cutover; the destination is in-process: the host links compose-core directly, no bridge / no socket)
host.ties_to_central: MotionDriver == cmMotion + advanceCmMotion + openK-interrupt; MenuState.on_hit == Central input-model click intents; surface_action == per-output Reuse/Recreate

compose_engine.port_data_role: compose_core.rs is a conformance MIRROR of the crate; scenes/menu-vertical.json is real-serde Scene JSON (Scene::from_json-loadable). See PARITY.md.
compose_engine.thesis: one engine owns all chrome nodes, resolves tokens, composes in a single ordered pass — no node reads a peer's visibility
compose_engine.model: Scene { screen, nodes:[Node{ id, kind, rect, z, state, tokens, payload }] }
compose_engine.blob: managed node behind chrome menus/bar/dock; rect = element.inflate(DEFAULT_EDGE_MARGIN=6); PerfTier{Sdf->extruded, Static->rounded-rect} picks the rasterizer, never toggles the node; payload Blob{ backs }
compose_engine.reduce: reduce(ComposeInput)->Scene — single pass: dock(+blob), exactly-one bar(+blob), menu(blob FIRST chrome-only -> MenuPanel -> one MenuRow per hittable row)
compose_engine.proof: exactly 1 Bar + 1 MenuPanel & <=1 menu Blob across all styles · blob rect = panel.inflate(6) · blob excluded from input · hit at row centre -> MenuRow · in-crate unit tests + compose_core.rs parity_tests

input_model.types: CursorState{ x, y, over:Option<NodeId>, over_kind, over_z } · ClickIntent{ Awareness{over} | Away{surface} | Select{node} | Unfocus } · Transient{ Menu, Launcher, Sidebar }
input_model.track: CursorState::track(scene,x,y) — Scene::hit every move; topmost INTERACTIVE node (blobs/backings excluded)
input_model.resolve: resolve_click(scene,x,y,open,has_focus)->ClickIntent — pure+total; precedence away > select > unfocus > awareness
input_model.proof: exactly one intent per press · blob skirt never absorbs · engine owns focus + dismissal (no peer visibility toggles) · 6 input_tests in compose_core.rs

edge_aware.problem: reproducible across ALL surfaces — a deeper issue, not a per-surface offset: child affordances + hit/focus rects were placed in absolute space and never intersected against the parent surface's content box or the work-area, so they bleed past borders near any edge (the 'New' row outline + submenu bridge ran straight past the panel's right border).
edge_aware.fix: one geometry pass: Rect::intersect clips children to their surface; Rect::clamp_inside shifts (not shrinks) transient surfaces into the work-area; submenu_rect flushes to the panel edge and FLIPS to the opposite side at the screen edge. Hit-region and painted rect share one value.
edge_aware.api: Rect::intersect(parent) · Rect::clamp_inside(area, margin) · submenu_rect(panel, area, size, margin)->(Rect, Side{Left|Right})
edge_aware.proof: row ring never exceeds panel · submenu flips left at the right edge · panel shifted not shrunk · 6 edge_tests in compose_core.rs

diff_damage.thesis: retained-mode reactivity — state → reduce → new Scene → diff → targeted host updates; damage() gives the dirty region so a motion-only restyle never forces a full-screen repaint (E5).
diff_damage.diff: diff(prev,next)->SceneDiff — Removed first, then per-next Added | (Moved on rect change) + (Updated on state/z/tokens/payload); a move+restyle reports BOTH
diff_damage.damage: damage(prev,next)->Rect — union of changed rects (next rect; + prev rect when moved/removed); Rect::ZERO when nothing changed
diff_damage.proof: identical=empty/zero · move≠add/remove · restyle-in-place=Updated not Moved · move+restyle=both · motion-only damage < screen · moved damage unions old+new · 10 diff_tests in compose_core.rs

motion.mirror: motion_core.rs (port of src/motion.rs) — the non-rendering half of the motion engine
motion.types: Easing{ Linear|EaseOutCubic|EaseInOutCubic|Spring(Spring) } · Spring{ response_ms, damping_ratio } (soft=320/0.82) · MotionSpec{ duration_ms, easing, reduced_motion } (default ease_out 240) · MotionSample{ state, raw_progress, curve_progress, visible_progress }
motion.advance: advance_motion(state, elapsed_ms, spec) — Opening→Open, Closing→Hidden; spring curve may overshoot >1.0 while visible_progress stays input/opacity-safe 0..1
motion.interrupt: interrupt_motion(current, target, spec) — retarget in-flight WITHOUT a visible jump (monotonic easings preserve visible_progress via inverse_sample)
motion.proof: 6 motion tests (overshoot-safe, reduced-snap, closing-inverse, both interruption directions)

render.mirror: render_core.rs (port of src/render.rs) — the renderer abstraction (E6/M5)
render.thesis: a host holds a Renderer and never embeds a rasterizer; the renderer is a PURE CONSUMER of the scene model (paints what reduce produced, in z-order, from resolved tokens). Swapping tiny-skia ↔ wgpu/vello changes nothing about Scene/ids/tokens/input/motion.
render.types: RenderFrame{ scene, diff, damage, motion } · trait Renderer{ type Target; render(&mut self, frame, target) }
render.illustrative_backend: RecordingRenderer → DrawList[DrawCmd::RoundedRect] — host-side pattern (real backends bring tiny-skia/wgpu); emits one primitive per live node in back-to-front z-order
render.proof: paints live nodes only · back-to-front z (100<200<300<305) · blob below the chrome it backs · scene unchanged after render · 3 render tests

menu_lifecycle_wired.note: Central's context menu is driven by the motion engine (advance_motion) — cm.p IS visible_progress; openK retargets in-flight via interrupt_motion so closing mid-open reverses without a jump (easeOut 220ms). Other surfaces keep the physical spring solver (velocity-carrying, also interruption-smooth).

dock.note: the dock has two real, orthogonal backend layers — both kept, neither subsumes the other

visibility_layer.model: DockSettings { enabled, archived, mode, reveal_delay_ms, hide_delay_ms, pinned }
visibility_layer.resolve: resolve(DockContext{ window_overlaps, edge_hovered, pointer_in_dock }) -> DockResolution{ painted, visible, reserves_zone, reveal_strip }
visibility_layer.proof: docked reserves / floating does not · autohide hidden until edge-hover · dodge hides only under overlap · pinned always visible+reserves · 6 tests

layout_layer.model: DockAffordanceConfig { deck_style: Row|Stack|Fan, card_px, fan_arc_deg, spread, items }

dock_affordance.config: DockAffordanceConfig { deck_style, card_px, fan_arc_deg, spread, items }
dock_affordance.proof: one config -> N CardPlacement via place(); no per-style host path (deck of cards without rewriting the runtime)

taskbar_registry.unknown_id: resolves to None -> surfaced diagnostic, never silently dropped

motion_spec.consumes: scene_motion.rs maps eases→gpui bezier closures, engine_springs→compose-core solver
